text: Jazakallahu khairan (May Allah reward you with good).
footnote: Reported by At-Tirmidhi no. 2035. See Sahih Al-Jami' 6244 and Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/200

text: Whoever memorizes ten verses from the beginning of Surah Al-Kahf will be protected from the Dajjal. And seeking refuge in Allah from his trial after the final Tashahhud of every prayer.
footnote: Muslim 1/555. In another narration, 'from the end of Al-Kahf' 1/556
See hadith no. 55 and hadith 56

text: May the One for whose sake you love me, love you.
footnote: Reported by Abu Dawud 4/333, and graded as hasan by Al-Albani in Sahih Sunan Abi Dawud 3/965

text: May Allah bless you in your family and your wealth.
footnote: Al-Bukhari, Al-Fath 4/88

text: May Allah bless you in your family and your wealth. The only reward for a loan is praise and repayment.
footnote: Reported by An-Nasa'i in 'Amal Al-Yawm wa Al-Laylah, p. 300, and Ibn Majah 2/809. See Sahih Ibn Majah 2/55

text: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from associating anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I do not know.
footnote: Ahmad 4/403 and others. See Sahih Al-Jami' 3/333 and Sahih At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib by Al-Albani

text: Wa feeka barakallah (And may Allah bless you too).
footnote: Reported by Ibn As-Sunni, p. 138, no. 278. See Al-Wabil As-Sayyib by Ibn Al-Qayyim, p. 304, verification by Bashir Muhammad 'Uyun

text: O Allah, there is no omen but Your omen, and no good but Your good, and no god other than You.
footnote: Ahmad 2/220 and Ibn As-Sunni no. 292. Authenticated by Al-Albani in Al-Ahadith As-Sahiha 3/54, no. 1065. As for good omens (fa'l), the Prophet (peace be upon him) used to like them; for this reason, he heard a good word from a man and it pleased him, so he said: 'We have taken your good omen from your mouth.' Abu Dawud and Ahmad, authenticated by Al-Albani in As-Sahiha 2/363 from Abu Ash-Shaykh in 'Akhlaq An-Nabi'

text: In the name of Allah. All praise is for Allah. {Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it. * And indeed, to our Lord we will surely return.} All praise is for Allah, all praise is for Allah, all praise is for Allah. Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest. Glory be to You, O Allah. I have wronged myself, so forgive me, for indeed, no one forgives sins except You.
footnote: Abu Dawud 3/34 and At-Tirmidhi 5/501. See Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/156

text: Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest. {Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it. * And indeed, to our Lord we will surely return.} O Allah, we ask You on this journey of ours for righteousness and piety, and for deeds that please You. O Allah, make this journey of ours easy for us and shorten its distance for us. O Allah, You are the Companion on the journey and the Guardian of the family. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from the gloom of the sights, and from a bad outcome in wealth and family. And when he returns, he says the same and adds to them: 'Returning, repenting, worshipping, and to our Lord praising.'
footnote: Narrated by Muslim 2/978

text: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and what they have shaded, and Lord of the seven earths and what they have carried, and Lord of the devils and what they have led astray, and Lord of the winds and what they have scattered. I ask You for the good of this village, the good of its people, and the good that is in it. And I seek refuge in You from its evil, the evil of its people, and the evil that is in it.
footnote: Al-Hakim, who authenticated it and Adh-Dhahabi agreed 2/100, and Ibn As-Sunni no. 524. Al-Hafiz graded it as hasan in Takhrij Al-Adhkar 5/154. Ibn Baz said: 'Narrated by An-Nasa'i with a good chain.' See Tuhfat Al-Akhyar, p. 37

text: There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him is all praise. He gives life and causes death, and He is the Ever-Living who does not die. In His hand is all good, and He is able to do all things.
footnote: At-Tirmidhi 5/491 and Al-Hakim 1/538. Graded as hasan by Al-Albani in Sahih Ibn Majah 2/21 and in Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/152

text: In the name of Allah.
footnote: Abu Dawud 4/296, authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih Abi Dawud 3/941

text: I entrust you to Allah, whose trusts are never lost.
footnote: Ahmad 2/403 and Ibn Majah 2/943. See Sahih Ibn Majah 2/133

text: I entrust to Allah your religion, your trusts, and the last of your deeds.
May Allah provide you with piety, forgive your sin, and make goodness easy for you wherever you are.
footnote: Ahmad 2/7, At-Tirmidhi 5/499. See Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/155
At-Tirmidhi. See Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/155

text: Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'When we ascended, we would say 'Allahu Akbar,' and when we descended, we would say 'Subhanallah.''
footnote: Al-Bukhari, Al-Fath 6/135

text: A hearer has heard our praise of Allah and His good trial upon us. Our Lord, accompany us and bestow Your grace upon us, seeking refuge in Allah from the Fire.
footnote: Muslim 4/2086. The meaning of 'a hearer has heard' is: a witness has witnessed our praise of Allah for His blessings and good trial. And the meaning of 'a hearer has heard' is: let a hearer convey this statement of mine to others and say the like, as a reminder for remembrance at dawn and supplication. And his saying: 'Our Lord, accompany us and bestow Your grace upon us' means: protect us, surround us, guard us, and bestow upon us Your abundant blessings and turn away from us all harm. Sharh An-Nawawi 17/39

text: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
footnote: Narrated by Muslim 4/2080

text: He says 'Allahu Akbar' three times on every high place, then says: 'There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. To Him belongs the dominion, and to Him is all praise, and He is able to do all things. Returning, repenting, worshipping, and to our Lord praising. Allah has fulfilled His promise, and helped His servant, and defeated the confederates alone.'
footnote: The Prophet (peace be upon him) used to say it when returning from a battle or Hajj. Al-Bukhari 7/163 and Muslim 2/980

text: When something pleasing came to the Prophet (peace be upon him), he would say: 'All praise is for Allah, by whose grace good deeds are completed.' And if something displeasing came to him, he would say: 'All praise is for Allah in all circumstances.'
footnote: Reported by Ibn As-Sunni in 'Amal Al-Yawm wa Al-Laylah and Al-Hakim, who authenticated it 1/499. Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih Al-Jami' 4/201

text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever sends one prayer upon me, Allah will send ten prayers upon him.'
And the Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'Do not make my grave a place of festival, but send prayers upon me, for your prayers will reach me wherever you are.'
And the Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'The miser is the one in whose presence I am mentioned and he does not send prayers upon me.'
And the Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'Indeed, Allah has angels who travel throughout the earth, conveying to me the greetings of peace from my Ummah.'
And the Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'No one sends greetings of peace upon me but Allah returns my soul to me so that I may return the greeting of peace.'
footnote: Reported by Muslim 1/288
Abu Dawud 2/218 and Ahmad 2/367. Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih Abi Dawud 2/383
At-Tirmidhi 5/551 and others. See Sahih Al-Jami' 3/25 and Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/177
An-Nasa'i, and Al-Hakim 2/421. Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih An-Nasa'i 1/274
Abu Dawud no. 2041, graded as hasan by Al-Albani in Sahih Abi Dawud 1/383

text: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'You will not enter Paradise until you believe, and you will not believe until you love one another. Shall I not guide you to something which, if you do it, you will love one another? Spread Salam among yourselves.'
Three things, whoever combines them has combined faith: fairness from yourself, offering Salam to the world, and spending from what is little.

On the authority of 'Abdullah bin 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with them): A man asked the Prophet (peace be upon him), 'What is the best of Islam?' He said: 'To feed the poor and to greet with Salam those you know and those you do not know.'
footnote: Muslim 1/74 and others
Al-Bukhari with Fath 1/82 from 'Ammar (may Allah be pleased with him), as a mawquf, mu'allaq narration
Al-Bukhari with Fath 1/55 and Muslim 1/65

text: If the People of the Book greet you, then say: 'Wa 'alaykum' (And upon you).
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 11/42, and Muslim 4/1705

text: When you hear the crowing of roosters, ask Allah for His bounty, for they have seen an angel. And when you hear the braying of a donkey, seek refuge in Allah from Satan, for it has seen a devil.
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 6/350, and Muslim 4/2092

text: When you hear the barking of dogs and the braying of donkeys at night, seek refuge in Allah from them, for they see what you do not see.
footnote: Abu Dawud 4/327 and Ahmad 3/306. Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih Abi Dawud 3/961

text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'O Allah, whichever believer I have cursed, make that a means of nearness to You for him on the Day of Resurrection.'
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 11/171 and Muslim 4/2007, whose wording is 'make it for him a purification and a mercy.'

text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'If one of you must praise his companion, let him say: 'I consider so-and-so to be such-and-such, and Allah is his reckoner, and I do not sanctify anyone before Allah. I consider him to be - if he knows that - such-and-such.''
footnote: Narrated by Muslim 4/2296

text: O Allah, do not hold me accountable for what they say, and forgive me for what they do not know, [and make me better than what they think].
footnote: Al-Bukhari in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad, no. 761. Al-Albani authenticated its chain in Sahih Al-Adab Al-Mufrad, no. 585. What is between the brackets is an addition by Al-Bayhaqi in Shu'ab Al-Iman 4/228 from another route.

text: Labbayka Allahumma labbayk, labbayka la sharika laka labbayk. Innal-hamda wan-ni'mata laka wal-mulk, la sharika lak. (Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Indeed, all praise, grace, and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.)
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 3/408, and Muslim 2/841

text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) circumambulated the House on a camel. Every time he came to the Corner, he would point to it with something he had and say 'Allahu Akbar'.
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 3/476. The 'something' refers to a crooked staff. See: Al-Bukhari with Fath 3/472

text: {Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.}
footnote: Abu Dawud 2/179, Ahmad 3/411, and Al-Baghawi in Sharh As-Sunnah 7/128. Graded as hasan by Al-Albani in Sahih Abi Dawud 1/354. The verse is from Surah Al-Baqarah, 201.

text: When the Prophet (peace be upon him) approached As-Safa, he recited: {Indeed, As-Safa and Al-Marwah are among the symbols of Allah.} 'I begin with what Allah began with.' So he began with As-Safa, climbing it until he could see the Ka'bah. He faced the Qiblah, declared Allah's Oneness and greatness, and said: 'There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him is all praise, and He is able to do all things. There is no god but Allah, alone. He fulfilled His promise, and helped His servant, and defeated the confederates alone.' Then he supplicated between that. He said this three times. The hadith continues: 'He did on Al-Marwah as he had done on As-Safa.'
footnote: Muslim 2/888

text: The best of supplications is the supplication on the Day of 'Arafah, and the best of what I and the Prophets before me have said is: 'There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him is all praise, and He is able to do all things.'
footnote: At-Tirmidhi, graded as hasan by Al-Albani in Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/184, and in As-Silsilah As-Sahiha 4/6

text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) rode Al-Qaswa' until he reached Al-Mash'ar Al-Haram. He faced the Qiblah, (supplicated to Him, said 'Allahu Akbar,' 'La ilaha illallah,' and declared His Oneness) and remained standing until it was very bright. He then departed before the sun rose.
footnote: Muslim 2/891

text: He says 'Allahu Akbar' each time he throws a pebble at the three Jamarat. Then he moves forward, stands, and supplicates facing the Qiblah, raising his hands after the first and second Jamrah. As for Jamrat Al-'Aqabah, he stones it and says 'Allahu Akbar' with each pebble, then departs without standing there.
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 3/583 and 3/584, see its wording there. And Al-Bukhari with Fath 3/581, also narrated by Muslim.

text: Subhanallah (Glory be to Allah)
Allahu Akbar (Allah is the greatest)
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 1/210, 390, 414 and Muslim 4/1857
Al-Bukhari, Al-Fath 8/441. See Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/103 and 2/235, and Musnad Ahmad 5/218

text: When something pleasing or delightful came to the Prophet (peace be upon him), he would prostrate in gratitude to Allah, the Blessed and Exalted.
footnote: Narrated by the compilers of Sunan except An-Nasa'i. See Sahih Ibn Majah 1/233 and Irwa' al-Ghalil 2/226

text: Place your hand on the part of your body that hurts and say: 'Bismillah' (In the name of Allah) three times. And say seven times: 'A'udhu billahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhir' (I seek refuge in Allah and His power from the evil of what I feel and what I fear).
footnote: Muslim 4/1728

text: If one of you sees something from his brother, or from himself, or from his wealth that he likes, [let him pray for blessings for it], for the evil eye is real.
footnote: Musnad Ahmad 4/447, Ibn Majah, and Malik. Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih Al-Jami' 1/212. See the verification of Zad Al-Ma'ad by Al-Arna'ut 4/170

text: La ilaha illallah (There is no god but Allah).
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 6/181, and Muslim 4/2208

text: Bismillah, Allahu Akbar. [O Allah, from You and for You.] O Allah, accept from me.
footnote: Muslim 3/1557 and Al-Bayhaqi 9/287. What is between the brackets is from Al-Bayhaqi 9/287 and others. The last sentence is conveyed in meaning from the narration of Muslim.

text: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah, which no righteous or wicked person can transgress, from the evil of what He has created, brought into existence, and spread, from the evil of what descends from the sky, from the evil of what ascends therein, from the evil of what is created in the earth, from the evil of what comes out of it, from the evil of the trials of the night and day, and from the evil of every visitor except a visitor who comes with good, O Most Merciful.
footnote: Ahmad 3/419 with an authentic chain, and Ibn As-Sunni no. 637. Al-Arna'ut authenticated its chain in his verification of At-Tahawiyyah, p. 133. See Majma' Az-Zawa'id 10/127.

text: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'By Allah, I seek the forgiveness of Allah and repent to Him more than seventy times a day.'
And he (peace be upon him) said: 'O people, repent to Allah, for I repent to Him one hundred times a day.'

And he (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever says: 'I seek the forgiveness of Allah the Great, other than whom there is no god, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining, and I repent to Him,' Allah will forgive him, even if he has fled from the battlefield.'
And he (peace be upon him) said: 'The closest the Lord is to a servant is in the last part of the night, so if you can be among those who remember Allah at that hour, then do so.'
And he (peace be upon him) said: 'The closest a servant is to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so increase your supplications.'
And he (peace be upon him) said: 'Verily, at times there is a covering over my heart, and I seek the forgiveness of Allah one hundred times a day.'
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 11/101
Muslim 4/2076

Reported by Abu Dawud 2/85, At-Tirmidhi 5/569, and Al-Hakim who authenticated it and Adh-Dhahabi agreed 1/511. Authenticated by Al-Albani. See Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/182 and Jami' Al-Usul li-Ahadith Ar-Rasul (peace be upon him) 4/389-390 with the verification of Al-Arna'ut.
Reported by At-Tirmidhi, An-Nasa'i 1/279, and Al-Hakim. See Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/183 and Jami' Al-Usul with the verification of Al-Arna'ut 4/144.
Muslim 1/350

Reported by Muslim 4/2075. Ibn Al-Athir said: '(A covering over my heart)' means it is covered or veiled. What is meant is forgetfulness; because he (peace be upon him) was always in a state of increasing remembrance, nearness, and constant watchfulness, so if he forgot something of it at times, or was forgetful, he counted it as a sin upon himself and rushed to seek forgiveness. See Jami' Al-Usul 4/386.

text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever says 'Subhanallahi wa bihamdihi' (Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him) one hundred times a day, his sins will be erased, even if they are like the foam of the sea.'

And he (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever says 'La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu, wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir' (There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him is all praise, and He is able to do all things) ten times, it will be as if he freed four souls from the descendants of Ishmael.'

And he (peace be upon him) said: 'Two words that are light on the tongue, heavy in the balance, and beloved to the Most Merciful: 'Subhanallahi wa bihamdihi, Subhanallahil-'Azim' (Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him, Glory be to Allah the Great).'

And he (peace be upon him) said: 'For me to say 'Subhanallah, walhamdulillah, wa la ilaha illallah, wallahu akbar' (Glory be to Allah, and praise be to Allah, and there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the greatest) is more beloved to me than all that the sun has risen upon.'

And he (peace be upon him) said: 'Is one of you unable to earn a thousand good deeds every day?' A questioner from his companions asked: 'How can one of us earn a thousand good deeds?' He said: 'He says 'Subhanallah' one hundred times, and a thousand good deeds will be written for him, or a thousand sins will be erased from him.'
Whoever says 'Subhanallahil-'Azim wa bihamdihi' (Glory be to Allah the Great and praise be to Him), a palm tree will be planted for him in Paradise.

And he (peace be upon him) said: 'O 'Abdullah bin Qais, shall I not guide you to a treasure from the treasures of Paradise?' I said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'Say 'La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah' (There is no might nor power except with Allah).'
And he (peace be upon him) said: 'The most beloved words to Allah are four: 'Subhanallah, walhamdulillah, wa la ilaha illallah, wallahu akbar.' It does not matter which of them you start with.'

A Bedouin came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and said: 'Teach me some words to say.' He said: 'Say: 'La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, Allahu Akbaru kabira, walhamdulillahi kathira, Subhanallahi Rabbil-'alamin, la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahil-'Azizil-Hakim' (There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. Allah is the greatest, immensely. And all praise is due to Allah, abundantly. Glory be to Allah, Lord of the worlds. There is no might nor power except with Allah, the Mighty, the Wise).' He said: 'These are for my Lord, but what is for me?' He said: 'Say: 'Allahummaghfir li, warhamni, wahdini, warzuqni' (O Allah, forgive me, have mercy on me, guide me, and provide for me).'

When a man embraced Islam, the Prophet (peace be upon him) would teach him the prayer, then command him to supplicate with these words: 'Allahummaghfir li, warhamni, wahdini, wa 'afini, warzuqni' (O Allah, forgive me, have mercy on me, guide me, grant me well-being, and provide for me).'
Indeed, the best remembrance is 'La ilaha illallah.'
The enduring good deeds are: 'Subhanallah, walhamdulillah, wa la ilaha illallah, wallahu akbar, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah.'

text: When the wings of the night spread – or when you reach the evening – keep your children in, for the devils spread out at that time. Then when an hour of the night has passed, let them go. And close the doors and mention the name of Allah, for Satan does not open a closed door. And tie up your waterskins and mention the name of Allah, and cover your vessels and mention the name of Allah, even if you only place something across it, and extinguish your lamps.
footnote: Al-Bukhari with Fath 10/88 and Muslim 3/1595
